title: pyrus pashia
intro: Pyrus pashia, also known as the Himalayan apple or Bihor, is a deciduous shrub or small tree species native to the Himalayan and Eastern Asian regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Pyrus pashia include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, Pyrus pashia is often used in the form of decoctions, infusions, and extracts, and is also made into powders, tinctures, and ointments for topical application.
